comment.body:
high volume areas mark the areas where the most participants are agreed... the auction succeeds there, liquidity is higher. 
vice versa

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
thats why traders, funds etc. calculate things like VWAP, AVWAP... that are volume weighted average prices...
so you get all prices touched on that day, take their volume, and naturally the higher volume levels will get higher weights, so the average price will skewed towards these levels... or even "clusters"
comment.raw_body:

comment.body:
options market is not the only market ;))
i will write a new educational post, bcs I see ppl getting lost in options a lot. thanks to greek furus.
thats why i haven't started my educational post series yet about greeks, bcs i want to make you see that the market is bigger than BSM model.

it is all about structure. have you every seen a liquidity heatmap before? that that like Bookmap presents? 
Now, what options market is doing is that it BETS on liquidity... they bet on what they expect to be realized. 
option can bet on time and volatility. thats the key. 
here volatility is how fast the spot moves basically.
